description: Strings parse must reject. malformed_checksum tampers the final checksum char of the ZNN token standard; bech32m_checksum is the ZNN core with a Bech32m checksum (same data part, different checksum constant) and must be rejected because token standards use the Bech32 variant, not Bech32m; uppercase_znn is the all-uppercase non-canonical ZNN string whose human-readable part decodes to ZTS, not zts; mixed_case_znn mixes upper and lower case, which Bech32 forbids; wrong_hrp_address is a canonical Zenon address (human-readable part z); wrong_hrp_zt has a valid checksum but the human-readable part zt; core_11_bytes and core_9_bytes are valid-checksum zts Bech32 strings whose decoded cores are 11 and 9 bytes instead of 10.
